recipes.recipe_card_markdown:
# Cauliflower Cheese Soup With Buttered Sourdough Croutons

Creamy cauliflower cheese soup blended with cheddar, hard cheese and milk, finished with olive oil, black pepper and dried thyme.

Serves: Luke, Sarah and Louis

Time: 40 minutes

Yield: 3 soup bowls

Meal role: cheap family dinner

## Ingredients

### Dairy

- Cowbelle Whole Milk 3.6% Fat - 500ml
- Emporium British Mature Cheddar Cheese - 150g
- Emporium Grated Hard Cheese - 80g
- Cowbelle British Unsalted Butter - 20g

### Fruit/Veg

- Nature's Pick British Cauliflower - 1 whole cauliflower
- Nature's Pick Brown Onions - 1 medium onion
- Nature's Pick Garlic - 3 small cloves

### Pantry

- Specially Selected White Sourdough Sliced Loaf - 2 slices, about 120g total
- Solesta Extra Virgin Olive Oil - 1 1/2 tbsp (22.5ml) total: 3/4 tbsp (11.25ml) for the onion base, plus 2 1/4 tsp (11.25ml) for finishing
- Ready, Set... Cook! Dried Thyme - 2 tsp (2g) total: 1 tsp (1g) in the cauliflower bowl, plus 1 tsp (1g) for finishing
- Morrisons Dijon Mustard - 5g
- Solesta White Wine Vinegar - 5ml

## Equipment

- 32 cm tri-ply saute pan with lid
- 10 inch cast iron frying pan
- Nutribullet Smart Touch Blender
- Ladle
- Chopping board
- Knife
- Grater
- Spatula or wooden spoon

## Mise En Place

- Onion and garlic bowl: 1 medium onion chopped with 3 small garlic cloves sliced. Used in Step 1.
- Cauliflower bowl: 1 whole cauliflower cut into florets and small stalk pieces, with 1 tsp (1g) dried thyme. Used in Step 2.
- Cheese bowl: 150g grated cheddar, 80g hard cheese, 5g Dijon and 5ml white wine vinegar. Used in Step 4.
- Crouton bowl: 2 slices white sourdough diced with 20g butter ready by the cast iron pan. Used in Step 3.
- Serving setup: 3 soup bowls, 2 1/4 tsp (11.25ml) olive oil for finishing, 1 tsp (1g) dried thyme for finishing and the black pepper grinder. Used in Step 6.

## Method

### 1. Soften the onion and garlic

Heat the olive oil in the saute pan over medium heat. Add the onion and garlic with a pinch of salt. Cook for about 6-7 minutes until soft but not browned.

Uses:

- Solesta Extra Virgin Olive Oil - 3/4 tbsp (11.25ml)
- Onion and garlic bowl - all

### 2. Simmer the cauliflower

Add the cauliflower, milk, water and dried thyme. Bring to a gentle simmer, cover loosely and cook for about 18-20 minutes until the cauliflower stalk pieces are soft.

Uses:

- Cauliflower bowl - all
- Cowbelle Whole Milk 3.6% Fat - 500ml
- Water - enough to help the liquid cover the cauliflower, about 200ml

### 3. Make the buttered croutons

While the soup simmers, melt the butter in the cast iron pan. Add the diced sourdough and cook for about 5-6 minutes, tossing until golden and crisp.

Uses:

- Crouton bowl - all: 2 diced sourdough slices and 20g butter

### 4. Add cheese and blend smooth

Take the soup off the heat. Stir in the cheese bowl while the soup is still hot so the cheese softens into the cauliflower base. Blend smooth in the Nutribullet jug for about 1-2 minutes, then return the soup to the saute pan.

Uses:

- Simmered cauliflower, milk, onion and garlic soup base - all
- Cheese bowl - all

### 5. Warm and season the soup

Warm the blended soup gently on low heat for about 2-3 minutes until glossy and hot enough to serve. Do not boil hard after the dairy goes in, because hard boiling can make cheese split or turn grainy. Season with salt and black pepper to taste.

Uses:

- Blended cauliflower cheese soup - all

### 6. Serve the dinner bowls

Ladle the finished soup into 3 bowls. Add croutons, then finish each bowl with a measured olive oil circle, black pepper and dried thyme.

Uses:

- Cauliflower cheese soup - all
- Buttered sourdough croutons - all
- Serving setup - 3 dinner bowls, olive oil finish: Luke 1 tsp (5ml), Sarah 3/4 tsp (3.75ml), Louis 1/2 tsp (2.5ml); dried thyme finish: 1 tsp (1g) scattered across the bowls to match bowl sizes; black pepper

## Plate

| Element | Luke | Sarah | Louis |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| Cauliflower Cheese Soup | 45% of the finished soup batch | 33% of the finished soup batch | 22% of the finished soup batch |
| Buttered Sourdough Croutons | 40% of the croutons | 35% of the croutons | 25% of the croutons |
| Olive Oil Thyme Finish | 1 tsp (5ml) olive oil + the largest thyme scatter | 3/4 tsp (3.75ml) olive oil + a medium thyme scatter | 1/2 tsp (2.5ml) olive oil + a small thyme scatter |

## Nutrition And Cost

| Person | Kcal | Protein | Carbs | Fat | Cost | Fullness |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| Luke | 941.3 | 49.6g | 50.6g | 61.6g | GBP 2.09 | 120.2 |
| Sarah | 714.6 | 37.0g | 40.0g | 46.1g | GBP 1.57 | 91.1 |
| Louis | 483.4 | 24.8g | 27.7g | 31.1g | GBP 1.07 | 61.6 |

## Cook's Notes

- The cheeses go into the hot soup before blending. This matches Luke's proven method and gives the smoothest texture.
- Blend the source-style soup volume once, then return it to the saute pan to warm gently.
- Keep the heat gentle after blending so the dairy stays smooth.
- Salt, black pepper and water are method helpers, so they are not counted in the shopping or nutrition rows.

recipes.recipe_card_markdown:
# Chicken Fajitas With Adult Lime Yoghurt Crema

Serves: Luke, Sarah, Louis

Time: 1477 minutes

Meal role: dinner

## Ingredients

### Protein

- Ashfields Chicken Drumstick Fillets - 600g

### Carbs

- Village Bakery Tortilla Protein Wrap 6 Pack - 600 count_x100

### Vegetables

- Nature's Pick Mixed Peppers - 320g
- Nature's Pick Brown Onions - 110g

### Sauce And Pantry

- Nature's Pick Garlic - 9g
- Stonemill Smoked Paprika - 5g
- Stonemill Ground Cumin - 5g
- Stonemill Dried Oregano - 1g
- Solesta Extra Virgin Olive Oil - 22.5ml
- Nature's Pick Limes 5 Pack - 150 count_x100

### Sauce

- Brooklea Fat Free Greek Style Yogurt - 160g

### Finish

- Ready, Set... Cook! British Cut Coriander - 11g

## Equipment

- 32 cm tri-ply saute pan with lid
- mixing bowl
- tongs
- basic utensils and chopping boards

## Mise En Place

- Chicken marinade bowl: Mix the marinade from 1 lime, 22.5ml olive oil, 3 small garlic cloves, 5g smoked paprika, 5g cumin, 1g oregano, salt and pepper. Mix the sliced chicken with the marinade and refrigerate ahead as a separate prep-ahead task, ideally the day before cooking. Keep the remaining 1/2 lime whole for the crema on cook day.
- Vegetable board: Slice the peppers and onion into thin strips so they char quickly.
- Mild crema bowl: Squeeze the remaining 1/2 lime into the yoghurt and stir until loose enough to spoon over all three plates.
- Wrap station: Set out six protein wraps. Luke gets 3 wraps, Sarah gets 2 wraps and Louis gets 1 wrap.
- Finish station: Keep 11g chopped coriander ready for plating.

## Method

### 1. Prep ahead: marinate the chicken

Mix the marinade from 1 lime, 22.5ml olive oil, 3 small garlic cloves, 5g smoked paprika, 5g cumin, 1g oregano, salt and pepper. Mix the sliced chicken with the marinade. Cover and chill for at least 1 hour. Keep the remaining 1/2 lime whole for the crema on cook day. Day-before prep is best when convenient, but this must not be scheduled as an exact 24-hour countdown. This is the prep-ahead task, not the cooking task.

Uses:

- Marinated chicken drum fillet strips - amount stated in this step: Ashfields Chicken Drumstick Fillets 600g
- Lime oil fajita seasoning pool - all: Nature's Pick Garlic 9g, Stonemill Smoked Paprika 5g, Stonemill Ground Cumin 5g, Stonemill Dried Oregano 1g, Solesta Extra Virgin Olive Oil 22.5ml, Nature's Pick Limes 5 Pack 150 count_x100

### 2. Make the mild crema

Squeeze the remaining 1/2 lime into the yoghurt and stir until tangy, mild and spoonable for all three plates.

Uses:

- Mild lime yoghurt crema - all: Brooklea Fat Free Greek Style Yogurt 160g

### 3. Char the chicken

Heat the 32 cm tri-ply saute pan until properly hot. Cook the 600g sliced chicken in one hot batch for about 7-8 minutes, leaving it alone at first so it chars before turning. Lift the cooked chicken out to a plate.

Uses:

- Marinated chicken drum fillet strips - amount stated in this step: Ashfields Chicken Drumstick Fillets 600g

### 4. Char the peppers and onion

Add the peppers and onion to the same hot pan. Cook for about 5-6 minutes until lightly charred but still with bite.

Uses:

- Charred peppers and onion - amount stated in this step: Nature's Pick Mixed Peppers 320g, Nature's Pick Brown Onions 110g

### 5. Combine and warm the wraps

Return the chicken to the pan for 1-2 minutes with the vegetables. Warm the six protein wraps one at a time in the dry pan.

### 6. Plate the fajita wraps

Serve this as fajita plates. Give Luke about half the finished fajita mix with 3 warm wraps, Sarah about one third with 2 warm wraps and Louis about one sixth with 1 warm wrap. Spoon over the mild crema, then add coriander.

Uses:

- Finished serving elements - as shown in the Plate table

## Plate

| Element | Luke | Sarah | Louis |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| Finished fajita mix | about half of the finished fajita mix, served with 3 wraps | about one third of the finished fajita mix, served with 2 wraps | about one sixth of the finished fajita mix, served with 1 wrap |
| Warm protein wraps | 3 warm protein wraps | 2 warm protein wraps | 1 warm protein wrap |
| Mild lime yoghurt crema | 75g crema | 60g crema | 25g crema |
| Coriander finish | 5g coriander | 4g coriander | 2g coriander |

## Nutrition And Cost

- Luke: 1079.9 kcal, 100.1g protein, 106.5g carbs, 28.5g fat, GBP 3.36
- Sarah: 723.9 kcal, 66.7g protein, 71.9g carbs, 18.6g fat, GBP 2.26
- Louis: 362.6 kcal, 33.7g protein, 35.4g carbs, 9.6g fat, GBP 1.14

## Cook's Notes

recipes.recipe_card_markdown:
# Ham Mushroom And Egg Breakfast Skillet

One-pan breakfast skillet with crisp-edged potatoes, mushrooms, chopped tomatoes, ham and soft-set eggs.

Serves: Luke, Sarah and Louis

Time: about 23 minutes

Yield: 3 breakfast plates

Meal role: breakfast

## Ingredients

### Protein

- Ham - 170g, roughly sliced or torn
- Free range mixed weight eggs - 5 eggs

### Carbs

- British white potatoes - 180g, cut into 1cm cubes

### Vegetables

- Closed cup mushrooms - 90g, sliced

### Sauce And Pantry

- Olive oil - 2 tbsp
- Chopped tomatoes - 90g

## Equipment

- 32 cm tri-ply saute pan with lid
- Chopping board and knife
- Small bowls or jug for the tomatoes and ham
- Spatula or fish slice
- Plates

## Mise En Place

- Potato board: 180g potatoes cut into 1cm cubes. Used in Step 1.
- Mushroom bowl: 90g sliced mushrooms. Used in Step 2.
- Tomato jug: 90g chopped tomatoes. Used in Step 3.
- Ham bowl: 170g roughly sliced or torn ham. Used in Step 3.
- Egg station: 5 eggs kept ready to crack straight into the skillet. Used in Step 4.

## Method

### 1. Start the potatoes

Heat the saute pan over medium heat for about 2 minutes. Add the oil, then add the potatoes and spread them out. Cook for about 9-11 minutes, turning every few minutes, until the cubes are golden at the edges and tender in the middle.

Uses:

- Olive oil - 2 tbsp, measured straight into the pan
- Potato board - all: 180g diced potatoes

### 2. Cook the mushrooms

Add the mushrooms to the pan and cook for about 3-4 minutes until they lose their moisture and start to brown.

Uses:

- Mushroom bowl - all: 90g sliced mushrooms

### 3. Make the skillet base

Pour in the chopped tomatoes, add the ham, and stir everything together. Cook for about 2-3 minutes until the tomatoes thicken enough to coat the potatoes and mushrooms. Grind in black pepper to taste.

Uses:

- Tomato jug - all: 90g chopped tomatoes
- Ham bowl - all: 170g ham

### 4. Set the eggs

Make five shallow wells in the skillet base and crack in the eggs. Cover with the lid and cook for about 4-5 minutes until the whites are set and the yolks are done to the family preference.

Uses:

- Egg station - all: 5 eggs

### 5. Plate

Spoon the skillet base onto the plates first, then lift the eggs on top. Luke gets about 36% of the skillet base with 2 eggs. Sarah gets about 35% of the skillet base with 2 eggs. Louis gets about 30% of the skillet base with 1 egg. Serve straight away while the potatoes still have crisp edges.

Uses:

- Ham mushroom tomato potato skillet base - all
- Soft set skillet eggs - all

## Plate

| Finished element | Luke | Sarah | Louis |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Ham mushroom tomato potato skillet base | about 36% of the finished skillet base | about 35% of the finished skillet base | about 30% of the finished skillet base |
| Soft set skillet eggs | 2 eggs | 2 eggs | 1 egg |

## Nutrition And Cost

- See the saved V3 portion guide and per-person data for the exact source-backed numbers.

## Cook's Notes

- Keep the potato cubes small and even so they cook through before the eggs go in.
- The pan needs to stay wide enough for one layer of potatoes and five shallow egg wells. Do not overcrowd it with extra ingredients.
- This is a one-pan fresh breakfast. It is not designed as a leftovers recipe.

recipes.recipe_card_markdown:
# Lemon Garlic Salmon With Potatoes And Green Beans

Simple roasted salmon with boiled potatoes and bright green beans, finished with lemon and garlic.

Serves: Luke, Sarah and Louis

Time: 30 minutes

Yield: 3 plates

Meal role: dinner

## Ingredients

### Protein

- Salmon fillets - 4 fillets, about 385g total

### Carbs

- British white potatoes - 540g total

### Vegetables

- Frozen whole green beans - 300g total

### Sauce And Pantry

- Lemons - 1 lemon, about 40g juice total
- Garlic - 3 small cloves, about 9g total
- Olive oil - 1 tbsp (15ml) total

## Equipment

- Single oven
- 20 cm saucepan with lid
- Mixing bowl
- Spoon, knife and plates

## Mise En Place

- Potato pan: 540g potatoes cut into even chunks so they finish together. Used in Steps 1 and 4.
- Lemon garlic oil bowl: 1 tbsp olive oil, 3 small garlic cloves finely grated, and the juice of 1 lemon mixed together. Used in Step 2.
- Salmon tray: 4 salmon fillets, about 385g total, set out in one layer on a lined tray. Used in Steps 2 and 4.
- Green bean bowl: 300g green beans ready to boil while the salmon roasts. Used in Steps 3 and 4.

## Method

### 1. Boil the potatoes

Put the Potato pan on the hob, cover the potatoes with cold water, bring to the boil, then simmer until tender. Start checking after about 12 minutes; most batches will take about 12-15 minutes.

Uses:

- Potato pan - all: 540g potatoes
- Water - enough to cover the potatoes

### 2. Roast the salmon

Heat the oven to 220C. Spoon the Lemon garlic oil bowl over the Salmon tray, turning the fillets so they are lightly coated, then roast until the salmon flakes easily. This usually takes about 14-16 minutes.

Uses:

- Lemon garlic oil bowl - all: 1 tbsp olive oil, 3 small garlic cloves and the juice of 1 lemon
- Salmon tray - all: 4 salmon fillets, about 385g total

### 3. Cook the green beans

When the salmon has about 5 minutes left, add the Green bean bowl to boiling water and cook until tender but still bright. Allow about 4-5 minutes, then drain well.

Uses:

- Green bean bowl - all: 300g green beans

### 4. Drain, finish and plate

Drain the potatoes, lift the salmon from the tray, and plate by finished serving elements. Add black pepper at the table, and serve Luke 170g salmon with 265g potatoes and 130g beans, Sarah 120g salmon with 160g potatoes and 100g beans, and Louis 95g salmon with 115g potatoes and 70g beans. This final dish-up should take about 2 minutes.

Uses:

- Boiled potatoes - all
- Lemon salmon and green beans - all
- Black pepper - a light grind at the table

## Plate

| Element | Luke | Sarah | Louis |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| Lemon salmon and green beans | 170g salmon with 130g green beans | 120g salmon with 100g green beans | 95g salmon with 70g green beans |
| Boiled potatoes | 265g potatoes | 160g potatoes | 115g potatoes |

## Nutrition And Cost

- Luke: 691.7 kcal, 42.8g protein, 58.2g carbs, 33.5g fat, GBP 3.08
- Sarah: 463.3 kcal, 29.8g protein, 36g carbs, 23.2g fat, GBP 2.15
- Louis: 348.7 kcal, 23.2g protein, 25.7g carbs, 17.6g fat, GBP 1.66

## Cook's Notes

- Boil the potatoes first so they can finish while the salmon roasts.
- Keep the lemon, garlic and oil as one small bowl so the salmon gets evenly coated without extra measuring at the hob.
- Black pepper is treated as a method helper, so it is added at the table instead of listed as a shopping ingredient.

recipes.recipe_card_markdown:
# Buttered Poached Eggs On White Sourdough Toast

Soft poached eggs on hot buttered white sourdough, seasoned simply at the plate.

Serves: Luke, Sarah and Louis

Time: 25 minutes

Yield: 3 plates

Meal role: breakfast

## Ingredients

### Dairy

- Merevale British Free Range Mixed Weight Eggs - 8 eggs
- Cowbelle British Unsalted Butter - 15g total

### Pantry

- Specially Selected White Sourdough Sliced Loaf - 3 slices, about 180g total

## Equipment

- 20 cm saucepan with lid
- Slotted spoon
- Toaster
- Plates

## Mise En Place

- Toast board: 3 slices of white sourdough with 15g unsalted butter ready beside them. Used in Step 2.
- Egg cups: 8 eggs, cracked into cups if you want cleaner poaching, or kept whole if cracking straight into the water. Used in Step 3.
- Finish station: salt and black pepper beside the plates. Used in Step 4.

## Method

### 1. Heat the poaching water

Bring the saucepan of water to a gentle simmer, then lower the heat so the water is moving but not bubbling hard. This usually takes about 5 minutes.

Uses:

- Water - enough for poaching

### 2. Toast and butter the sourdough

Toast the sourdough until golden and firm. Butter each slice while it is hot so the butter melts into the toast. This should take about 4 minutes.

Uses:

- Toast board - all: 3 slices white sourdough and 15g unsalted butter

### 3. Poach the eggs

Slide the eggs into the water in controlled rounds if the pan is crowded. Cook gently until the whites are set and the yolks are still soft. Allow about 3-4 minutes per round.

Uses:

- Egg cups - all: 8 eggs

### 4. Plate and season

Lift the eggs out with a slotted spoon and drain briefly. Plate using the table below, then finish each plate with salt and black pepper.

Uses:

- Buttered sourdough toast - all
- Poached eggs - all
- Finish station - salt and black pepper to taste

## Plate

| Element | Luke | Sarah | Louis |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| Buttered sourdough toast | 1 buttered slice | 1 buttered slice | 1 buttered slice |
| Poached eggs | 3 eggs | 3 eggs | 2 eggs |

## Nutrition And Cost

| Person | Kcal | Protein | Carbs | Fat | Cost | Fullness |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| Luke | 377.2 | 24.4g | 28.3g | 19.3g | GBP 0.86 | 95.1 |
| Sarah | 377.2 | 24.4g | 28.3g | 19.3g | GBP 0.86 | 95.1 |
| Louis | 310.1 | 17.9g | 28.3g | 14.7g | GBP 0.67 | 74.6 |

## Cook's Notes

- Butter the toast as soon as it comes out, then put the eggs on top.
- If the pan feels crowded, poach the eggs in two or three controlled rounds.
- Plate Sarah and Louis first if useful, then finish Luke's 3-egg plate.
- Salt and black pepper are neutral helpers, so they are mentioned in the method but not counted in the shopping or nutrition rows.

recipes.recipe_card_markdown:
# Salmon Teriyaki Rice And Greens

Serves: Luke, Sarah, Louis

Time: 35 minutes

Meal role: dinner

## Ingredients

### Protein

- Salmon fillets - 430g

### Carbs

- Long grain rice - 150g

### Vegetables

- Frozen broccoli florets - 270g

### Sauce

- Soy sauce - 30ml
- Clear honey - 20g
- Garlic - 9g
- Fresh ginger - 9g

### Sauce And Pantry

- Olive oil - 15ml total

## Equipment

- 20 cm Le Creuset tri-ply saucepan with lid
- 10 inch cast iron frying pan
- mixing bowl
- basic utensils and chopping boards

## Mise En Place

- Rice pan: rinse 150g long grain rice until the water runs mostly clear, then hold it in the pan with 270ml cold water ready for Step 1.
- Salmon plate: 4 salmon fillets, about 430g total, patted dry and ready for the frying pan. Used in Steps 2 and 4.
- Teriyaki bowl: mix 2 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tbsp clear honey, 3 small garlic cloves finely grated and 9g fresh ginger finely grated until the honey loosens. Used in Step 3.
- Broccoli bowl: 270g frozen broccoli florets ready to boil while the rice rests. Used in Steps 3 and 4.

## Method

### 1. Cook the rice

Put the Rice pan on the hob, bring it to the boil, cover, then cook on low until the grains are tender. This usually takes about 12-14 minutes. Turn off the heat and leave the lid on for 5 minutes so the rice can finish steaming.

Uses:

- Long grain rice - all: Long grain rice 150g

### 2. Sear the salmon

Heat the frying pan over a medium-high heat, add 2 tsp olive oil, then lay in the Salmon plate in one layer. Cook until the underside is deeply coloured, about 4-5 minutes, turn, and cook the second side until the fish flakes at the thickest point, about 2-3 minutes more. Lift the salmon to a warm plate.

Uses:

- Teriyaki salmon fillets - all: Salmon fillets 430g
- Salmon pan oil - all: Olive oil 10ml

### 3. Cook the broccoli and glaze

Boil the Broccoli bowl until bright green and tender, about 4 minutes, then drain well. Return the frying pan to medium heat, add 1 tsp olive oil, pour in the Teriyaki bowl, and bubble it for about 30-60 seconds until glossy. Tip in the drained broccoli, turn to coat, then spoon the glaze over the cooked salmon.

Uses:

- Bright broccoli - all: Frozen broccoli florets 270g
- Mild teriyaki-style sauce - all: Soy sauce 30ml, Clear honey 20g, Garlic 9g, Fresh ginger 9g
- Glaze pan oil - all: Olive oil 5ml

### 4. Plate the dinner

Fluff the rice, then plate by finished serving elements. Give Luke about half of the cooked rice with 178g salmon and 120g broccoli, Sarah a medium rice share with 140g salmon and 90g broccoli, and Louis the smaller remaining rice share with 112g salmon and 60g broccoli. The raw rice maths stays in the datasheet; the cooking card only needs the finished rice split. Serve straight away.

Uses:

- Finished serving elements - as shown in the Plate table

## Plate

| Element | Luke | Sarah | Louis |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| Teriyaki salmon and broccoli | 178g salmon with 120g broccoli | 140g salmon with 90g broccoli | 112g salmon with 60g broccoli |
| Long grain rice | 50% of the cooked rice | 30% of the cooked rice | 20% of the cooked rice |

## Nutrition And Cost

- Luke: 792.2 kcal, 47.3g protein, 77.7g carbs, 34.5g fat, GBP 3.04
- Sarah: 567.3 kcal, 36.1g protein, 48.4g carbs, 26.9g fat, GBP 2.35
- Louis: 427.3 kcal, 27.8g protein, 33.1g carbs, 21.3g fat, GBP 1.87

recipes.recipe_card_markdown:
# Buttery Scrambled Eggs On White Sourdough Toast

Serves: Luke, Sarah, Louis

Time: 18 minutes

Meal role: breakfast

## Ingredients

### Carbs

- Specially Selected White Sourdough Sliced Loaf - 180g

### Protein

- Merevale British Free Range Mixed Weight Eggs - 8 each

### Dairy

- Cowbelle British Unsalted Butter - 23g

### Finish


## Equipment

- 32 cm tri-ply saute pan with lid
- jug
- fork or small whisk
- silicone spatula
- toaster
- plates

## Mise En Place

- Toast board: Set out 3 slices of white sourdough and keep 15g of the butter ready for the toast.
- Egg jug: Crack 8 eggs into a jug, add a light pinch of salt and a light grind of black pepper per person, then whisk until the eggs are mostly combined.
- Pan butter dish: Keep 8g butter ready by the warmed saute pan so it can melt just before the egg jug goes in.

## Method

### 1. Warm the saute pan

Put the 32 cm tri-ply saute pan on the lowest heat for about 2-3 minutes so the base warms gently without browning the butter later.

### 2. Whisk the eggs

Crack the 8 eggs into the Egg jug, add the measured salt and black pepper, then whisk for about 1 minute until the yolks and whites are mostly combined.

Uses:

- Soft scrambled eggs - amount stated in this step: Merevale British Free Range Mixed Weight Eggs 8 each

### 3. Toast and butter the bread

Use the Toast board: put the 3 sourdough slices in the toaster. When they are done, butter each hot slice with 5g of the toast butter. This should take about 4 minutes.

Uses:

- Specially Selected White Sourdough Sliced Loaf - amount stated in this step: Specially Selected White Sourdough Sliced Loaf 180g
- Unsalted butter for toast and pan - amount stated in this step: Cowbelle British Unsalted Butter 23g

### 4. Cook the eggs softly

Use the Pan butter dish: melt 8g butter in the warm pan, pour in the Egg jug, then slowly scrape the bottom up with the spatula for about 4-5 minutes until the eggs are almost cooked but still soft.

Uses:

- Soft scrambled eggs - amount stated in this step: Merevale British Free Range Mixed Weight Eggs 8 each
- Unsalted butter for toast and pan - amount stated in this step: Cowbelle British Unsalted Butter 23g

### 5. Finish off the heat

Take the pan off the heat while the eggs are still slightly glossy. Finish the toast, then spoon the scrambled eggs over the buttered slices by finished serving element: Luke and Sarah get about two fifths each, and Louis gets about one quarter. This should take about 1 minute.

Uses:

- Soft scrambled eggs - amount stated in this step: Merevale British Free Range Mixed Weight Eggs 8 each
- Specially Selected White Sourdough Sliced Loaf - amount stated in this step: Specially Selected White Sourdough Sliced Loaf 180g
- Unsalted butter for toast and pan - amount stated in this step: Cowbelle British Unsalted Butter 23g

## Plate

| Element | Luke | Sarah | Louis |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| Buttered Sourdough Toast | 1 buttered slice | 1 buttered slice | 1 buttered slice |
| Soft Scrambled Eggs | about two fifths of the cooked egg batch | about two fifths of the cooked egg batch | about one quarter of the cooked egg batch |

## Nutrition And Cost

- Luke: 413.8 kcal, 25.8g protein, 28.2g carbs, 22.9g fat, GBP 0.87
- Sarah: 413.8 kcal, 25.8g protein, 28.2g carbs, 22.9g fat, GBP 0.87
- Louis: 334.3 kcal, 18.9g protein, 28.2g carbs, 17.1g fat, GBP 0.68

# Strawberry Banana Fat-Free Greek Yoghurt Bowl With Honey And Chia

Fresh strawberry and banana bowls with fat-free Greek-style yoghurt, measured honey and chia.

Serves: Luke, Sarah and Louis

Time: 8 minutes

Yield: 3 bowls

Meal role: breakfast

## Ingredients

### Dairy

- Brooklea Fat Free Greek Style Yogurt - 730g total

### Fruit/Veg

- Nature's Pick Bananas - 2 bananas
- Nature's Pick Strawberries - 140g

### Pantry

- Everyday Essentials Clear Honey - 15g total
- Chia seeds - 15g total

## Equipment

- 3 breakfast bowls
- Chopping board
- Knife
- Scales
- Spoon

## Mise En Place

- Fruit board: 2 bananas sliced and 140g strawberries washed, hulled and sliced. Used in Step 1 and Step 3.
- Bowl set: 3 breakfast bowls on the scales with the yoghurt tub and spoon ready. Used in Step 2.
- Finish station: honey and chia seeds ready beside the bowls. Used in Step 4.

## Method

### 1. Prep the fruit

Wash and hull the strawberries, then peel and slice 2 bananas. This should take about 3 minutes.

Uses:

- Fruit board - all: 2 bananas and 140g strawberries

### 2. Spoon in the yoghurt

Spoon the yoghurt straight into the bowls.

Uses:

- Bowl set - yoghurt: Luke 290g, Sarah 260g, Louis 180g

### 3. Add the fruit

Share the sliced banana by weight, then add strawberries on top.

Uses:

- Fruit board - banana slices: Luke 85g, Sarah 60g, Louis 55g
- Fruit board - strawberries: Luke 50g, Sarah 50g, Louis 40g

### 4. Finish with honey and chia

Add honey and chia to each bowl. Serve straight away.

Uses:

- Finish station - 5g honey per bowl
- Finish station - 5g chia seeds per bowl

## Plate

| Element | Luke | Sarah | Louis |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| Greek yoghurt base | 290g | 260g | 180g |
| Fresh fruit | 85g banana + 50g strawberries | 60g banana + 50g strawberries | 55g banana + 40g strawberries |
| Honey and chia finish | 5g honey + 5g chia | 5g honey + 5g chia | 5g honey + 5g chia |

## Nutrition And Cost

| Person | Kcal | Protein | Carbs | Fat | Cost | Fullness |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| Luke | 302.4 | 19.2g | 52.7g | 2.5g | GBP 0.95 | 108.3 |
| Sarah | 263.5 | 17.1g | 44.9g | 2.4g | GBP 0.88 | 96.6 |
| Louis | 206.9 | 12.3g | 35.9g | 2.2g | GBP 0.68 | 71.6 |

## Cook's Notes

- Use 2 bananas total, sliced once, then weigh the banana into the bowls. This avoids a whole banana each and avoids leftover fruit.
- Louis eats the same as the adults here. Only spice or strong heat should be removed for him by default.
- Serve straight away so the banana stays fresh and the chia does not thicken the bowls too much.
- This is a lighter adult breakfast. The day planner must balance the adult protein gap later in the day.
